OFFICIAL PAPER OF CITY

Printing Contract Awarded by City Council After One of the
Hardest Battles in Its History ba

pages.raw_text:
President Hand had to rap for order.


Councilman Woydt, who introduced
the original motion that The Press
be given the contract on the ground
that II was the lowest reliable bidder,
started the ball rolling by offering
a motion that the rules be suspend
ed and that the matter of awarding
the contract for the city printing be
taken up.


His motion was seconded by Coun-
Gilman Funk. Councilman Pratt in
PRODUCED a substitute motion that the
Chronicle be given the contract, and
the Bulletin be named as second
paper instead of the Spokesman-Re-
view. Mr.. Pratt spoke at length in
favor of his amendment.


The vote on Mr.. Pratt's substitute
resolution was: Noes, Funk. Foster,
Johnson and Woydt; ayes. Burch,
Pratt and Hand.


Then Mr.. Woydt moved his original
motion to award the contract to The
Spokane Press. the motion made sew
eral nights ago, and the vote carried
by to as stated above.
